mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates compress the equity discount rate and stimulate borrowing-dependent corporate activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed cut rates to 0.09% by March 2020
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following months with FEDFUNDS below 1% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5%, it signals rapid labor market deterioration that reduces household income and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and fall in nonfarm payrolls captures a broad labor market collapse that depresses consumption and corporate revenue expectations more than either indicator alone, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises month-over-month and PAYEMS falls month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.9% and PAYEMS at 139435 in March 2020
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises month-over-month and PAYEMS falls month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When monetary policy is constrained at the zero lower bound, month-over-month declines in industrial production directly signal impaired corporate earnings capacity without prospect of rate cuts to offset the shock, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, month-over-month declines in INDPRO do not produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below one million units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream durable consumption that immediately reduces cyclical earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when HOUST is below 1000.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects accelerating consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month growth in RSAFS does not exhibit a positive rank correlation (Rank IC < 0.03) with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration that immediately raises equity risk premia and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns after UNRATE jumps greater than 0.3pp are positive in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is already near zero, a simultaneous decline in housing starts indicates that monetary stimulus cannot offset a collapsing residential investment sector, and the resulting credit contraction produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and HOUST at 956 in early 2020
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and declining HOUST does not produce negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).
